lines: +// TODO(wbpcode): The webtransport::Session should be used ideally. However, the

+   * Set a visitor for this WebTransport session. The visitor will be notified of session-level
+   * events such as incoming streams and datagrams. This is only used for negotiated WebTransport
+   * sessions, and should not be set for non-WebTransport sessions.
+   * @param visitor supplies the visitor to set.
enum class CodecType { HTTP1, HTTP2, HTTP3 };
+   * @return the WebTransport session of the *downstream* stream paired with this decoder, if any.
+   * Only decoders on the router's upstream path (which are paired 1:1 with a downstream stream)
+   * implement this; they reach the downstream StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks and return its
+   * webTransportSession(). It lets the upstream codec obtain the downstream WebTransport session so
+   * it can bridge the two directly. All other response decoders inherit the default empty OptRef.
* HTTP/3 streams return the HTTP/3 stream ID or nullopt if not available.
+   * @return the WebTransport session this stream carries, if it is a negotiated WebTransport
+   * CONNECT stream. Only HTTP/3 codec streams that have created a WebTransportHttp3 session return
+   * a value; all other streams inherit the default empty OptRef.
// Returns a handle to the upstream stream's stream info.

+  // if any. Used by the upstream WebTransport codec to obtain the downstream session so it can
+  // bridge the two. Defaults to an empty OptRef for non-WebTransport streams.
// Returns a handle to the generic upstream.
+   * @return the WebTransport session of the stream this decoder filter chain is bound to, if any.

* Continue iterating through the filter chain with buffered headers and body data. This routine
* can only be called if the filter has previously returned StopIteration from decodeHeaders()
void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, bool end_stream) override;
void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;
void addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) override;

* Called when the provided headers have been encoded by all the filters in the chain.
+  // Returns the WebTransport session of the codec stream this filter manager is bound to, if any.
+  // Delegates to the FilterManagerCallbacks (e.g. the HTTP connection manager's ActiveStream).
OptRef<const StreamInfo::StreamInfo> trackedStream() const override { return streamInfo(); }
void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const override {
+    // The base implementation may close the connection on invalid settings.
+  // The peer's SETTINGS (carrying WebTransport/HTTP Datagram negotiation) are now known. Write any
+  // WebTransport CONNECT requests whose encodeHeaders() was deferred because QUICHE only creates
+  // the upstream WebTransport session at header-write time and only if WebTransport is already
+  // negotiated. Move the set aside first so a flush that somehow re-registers (it should not, since
+  // settings_received() is now true) cannot mutate what we iterate.
+    // The stream may have been reset/closed while waiting; GetActiveStream() returns nullptr then,
+    // so a stale ID is harmless.
+  // Overridden to flush WebTransport CONNECT requests that were deferred until the peer's HTTP/3
+  // Registers/unregisters a client stream that buffered a WebTransport CONNECT request in
+  // encodeHeaders() because the peer's HTTP/3 SETTINGS had not yet been received. QUICHE creates
+  // the upstream WebTransport session synchronously inside WriteHeaders, but only if WebTransport
+  // is already negotiated (which is known only after SETTINGS). When the SETTINGS frame arrives,
+  // OnSettingsFrame() flushes every registered stream. Streams are tracked by ID rather than
+  // pointer: a stream that is destroyed without unregistering simply fails the GetActiveStream()
+  // lookup at flush time, so there is no dangling reference. Only WebTransport CONNECT streams ever
+  // wait here; all other requests are written immediately and the connection becomes ready right
+  // IDs of client streams that buffered a WebTransport CONNECT request awaiting the peer's HTTP/3
+  // SETTINGS. Flushed and cleared in OnSettingsFrame(). See registerStreamWaitingForSettings().
// These callbacks are owned by network filters and quic session should outlive
return absl::CancelledError("encodeHeaders is called on write-closed stream.");
+  // A WebTransport CONNECT can only be written once the peer's HTTP/3 SETTINGS have been received:
+  // QUICHE creates the upstream WebTransport session synchronously inside WriteHeaders, but only if
+  // WebTransport is already negotiated (SupportsWebTransport()), which is known only after
+  // SETTINGS. If SETTINGS haven't arrived yet, buffer the request and replay this method from
+  // flushPendingHeaders() when OnSettingsFrame() fires. Only this one stream waits; every other
+  // request on the connection is written immediately.
+    // The headers may have been transformed by UHV or not, so we check both the upgrade and
+      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, "Deferring WebTransport CONNECT until peer HTTP/3 SETTINGS received.",
+      // TODO(wbpcode): could we avoid this copy by keeping the reference only here because the
+      // request headers should never be released before the stream is closed?
-      // HTTP/3 Datagrams sent over CONNECT-UDP are already congestion controlled, so make it
+    // HTTP/3 Datagrams sent over CONNECT-UDP are already congestion controlled, so make it bypass
+    // The stream is being torn down while still waiting for the peer's SETTINGS to flush a deferred
+    // WebTransport CONNECT. Drop the buffered headers and unregister so the session does not flush
// TODO(https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/23564): Make the stream use Capsule Protocol
// for CONNECT-UDP support when the headers contain "Capsule-Protocol: ?1" or "Upgrade:
+    // A datagram visitor is already registered on this stream (e.g. the WebTransport session of
+    // QUICHE registered itself). Registering another would be a QUIC_BUG, so skip.
+        warn, "Skipping capsule protocol setup: a datagram visitor is already registered.", *this);
+  // After QUICHE processes the response headers for a WebTransport CONNECT, it has created the
+  // upstream WebTransportHttp3 session. Obtain the downstream session (created earlier, when the
+  // downstream CONNECT request arrived) from the paired downstream stream via the response decoder,
+  // and bridge the two directly here at the upstream — no filter state involved.
+  // web_transport() is non-null only for a negotiated WebTransport CONNECT (QUICHE created the
+  // session when these request headers were written). The 2xx check rejects WebTransport CONNECT
+  // installBridge creates the shared state and the two bridges that co-own it; each bridge nulls
+  // its side of the state when its session (and thus the bridge) is destroyed, so neither side ever
+  // forwards to a freed peer. This stream is itself the upstream Http::WebTransportSession (it
+  // implements the interface over its own web_transport()).
+  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, "Flushing deferred WebTransport CONNECT after peer HTTP/3 SETTINGS.",
+  // settings_received() is now true, so this replay writes the request instead of deferring again.
+  // Ignore the status: the original encodeHeaders() caller already received okStatus, and any error
+  // here (e.g. the stream was reset while waiting) is surfaced through the stream's reset
+  // Writes a WebTransport CONNECT request whose encodeHeaders() was deferred until the peer's
+  // HTTP/3 SETTINGS arrived (see encodeHeaders()). Called by
+  // EnvoyQuicClientSession::OnSettingsFrame(). A no-op if no request is pending.
absl::optional<uint32_t> codecStreamId() const override { return id(); }
+  // Exposes this stream's WebTransport session (created by QUICHE for a negotiated WebTransport
+  // CONNECT) through the codec Stream interface, mirroring EnvoyQuicServerStream. Lets tests reach
+  // the session via Http::Stream. Empty OptRef when this is not a WebTransport stream.
void OnMetadataComplete(size_t frame_len, const quic::QuicHeaderList& header_list) override;
// Makes the QUIC stream use Capsule Protocol. Once this method is called, any calls to encodeData
// are expected to contain capsules which will be sent along as HTTP Datagrams. Also, the stream
// starts to receive HTTP/3 Datagrams and decode into Capsules.
+  // Returns true if the capsule protocol handler was installed. Returns false (without installing

+  // session of QUICHE), since registering a second one would be a QUIC_BUG.
+  // If the headers indicate this is a WebTransport session, initialize the WebTransportSessionState

// Returns nullptr if the response decoder has already been destructed.
// When an HTTP Upgrade is requested, this contains the protocol upgrade type, e.g. "websocket".
// It will be empty, when no such request is active.
